Urinalysis

Test12-JUN-2025Reference RangeUnit

Nitrite [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine nitrite positive
Hemoglobin [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine blood test = +

Leukocytes [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field47.8 H0 - 5/[HPF]
Erythrocytes [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field1.10 - 5/[HPF]
Epithelial cells [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field1.10 - 5/[HPF]

Mucus [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopyMucus in urine
Bacteria [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power fieldFinding of presence of bacteria

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Urinalysis shows significant leukocyturia, positive nitrites, bacteriuria, and >100,000 CFU/mL E. coli, indicating a urinary tract infection. Presence of hematuria and proteinuria is noted. Low specific gravity may suggest dilute urine. No ketones or casts detected. Recommend clinical correlation and consideration of antibiotic therapy based on sensitivity testing.

coding.display: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
